title: But Who Shall See. (Air.--Stevenson.)
content:
But who shall see the glorious day
        When, throned on Zion's brow,
    The LORD shall rend that veil away
        Which hides the nations now?[1]
    When earth no more beneath the fear
        Of this rebuke shall lie;[2]
    When pain shall cease, and every tear
        Be wiped from every eye.[3]

    Then, Judah, thou no more shall mourn
        Beneath the heathen's chain;
    Thy days of splendor shall return,
        And all be new again.[4]

    The Fount of Life shall then be quaft
        In peace, by all who come;[5]
    And every wind that blows shall waft
        Some long-lost exile home.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Thomas Moore, titled "But Who Shall See. (Air.--Stevenson.)", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
